source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 4. ADD(HAD1417MCW) Dimdi 등록관련 기술문서 제출 220215/D TCF/Attachment 08. Software Validation Report/8-2 Software IEC 62304 checklist (A-TCF-5-047).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 62304 software lifecycle checklist for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector model ADD.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation and technical documentation by mapping IEC 62304 lifecycle, risk management, configuration management, maintenance, and problem resolution requirements to supporting document A-TCF-5-046.
keyPoints: Document No. A-TCF-5-047, Rev. No. 1, Rev. Date 2021.04.02.
Product Name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and Model Name is ADD.
The checklist includes IEC 62304 clauses for software development, maintenance, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution.
Risk management process requirements cite ISO 14971.
Supporting document references repeatedly identify A-TCF-5-046 as Reference Software Lifecycle Process.

concepts.name: Software safety classification
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C) according to the possible effects on the patient, operator or other people resulting from a HAZARD to which the SOFTWARE SYSTEM can contribute.

concepts.name: ISO 14971 risk management
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: The checklist includes a risk management process requirement complying with ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Software safety classes A, B, and C are assigned according to possible effects from hazards to which the software system can contribute.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C) according to the possible effects on the patient, operator or other people resulting from a HAZARD to which the SOFTWARE SYSTEM can contribute.

evidence.claim: Class A means no injury or damage to health is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: No injury or damage to health is possible

evidence.claim: Class B means non-serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class B: Non-SERIOUS INJURY is possible

evidence.claim: Class C means death or serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class C: Death or SERIOUS INJURY is possible

evidence.claim: The checklist references A-TCF-5-046 as Reference Software Lifecycle Process.
The checklist covers software development process requirements.
The checklist covers software maintenance process requirements.
The checklist covers software risk management process requirements.
The checklist covers software configuration management process requirements.
The checklist covers software problem resolution process requirements.
SOUP-related requirements are marked not applicable in multiple checklist entries.
evidence.evidenceText: If a SOFTWARE ITEM is identified as SOUP, the MANUFACTURER has specified functional and performance requirements for the SOUP item that are necessary for its intended use. X X Not applicable

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the document body; source metadata says authoritySignal MFDS/domestic while the source path includes CE MDD and Dimdi.
The source path and productSignal mention HAD1417MCW and HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the document body only states Model Name ADD.
OCR/text extraction contains repeated headers, duplicated page fragments, and minor OCR issues such as 'PROCESESS', 'developmen', 'Lift Cycle', and 'obsolesence'.
